title: EU AI Act implementation resources
description: Official implementation materials, codes of practice, guidance, and policy updates related to the European Union AI Act.

metadata.license_status: Official public web source; reuse terms require review
review_checks: Known overlap with existing CEI EU records
known_gaps: Confirm reuse terms before storing full text
Map implementation updates to existing statement keys

live.meta_description: The AI Act is the first-ever legal framework on AI, which addresses the risks of AI and positions Europe to play a leading role globally.

title: NIST AI Risk Management Framework resources
publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
description: Framework, playbook, profiles, crosswalks, and supporting resources for voluntary AI risk management practice.

metadata.license_status: US government public source; citation and downstream reuse review still required
review_checks: Resource family grouped under one source
Existing CEI NIST records likely matchable
known_gaps: Split framework, profiles, and playbook into separate records
Capture version dates for each PDF resource

title: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of AI
description: Global AI ethics standard, implementation resources, readiness methodology, and impact assessment materials from UNESCO.

metadata.license_status: Official public web source; publication reuse terms require review
review_checks: Implementation methods noted as separate artifacts
known_gaps: Separate recommendation text from implementation tools

live.meta_description: UNESCO promotes ethical AI through global recommendations, guiding responsible design, development, and use of artificial intelligence.

title: OECD.AI Policy Navigator
description: Living repository of national and international AI policy initiatives maintained by OECD.AI contributors and experts.

metadata.license_status: Public web source; data reuse terms require review
review_checks: Candidate duplicate matching against existing CEI records

live.meta_description: OECD.AI helps countries and shape trustworthy AI with the OECD AI Principles. It gives access to 900+ national AI policies and initiatives, live data about AI and a blog about AI policy.

title: Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI
description: Official treaty page for the Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence, Human Rights, Democracy and the Rule of Law.
coverage: Council of Europe and treaty signatories

metadata.license_status: Official public web source; treaty text reuse terms require review
review_checks: High-value provenance target for legal instruments
known_gaps: Capture signatory status separately from treaty text

title: Global Index on Responsible AI
description: Country-level responsible AI index tracking policy, capability, and safeguard indicators across a broad global sample.

review_checks: Coverage normalized as country-level comparative data
known_gaps: Confirm whether record-level data is downloadable
Keep index indicators separate from source policy records

live.page_title: The Global Index on Responsible AI
live.meta_description: Igniting global action on responsible AI, with local evidence. The Global Index on Responsible AI, a multidimensional tool measuring progress towards responsible AI in 138 countries.

title: CAIDP Artificial Intelligence and Democratic Values Index
publisher: Center for AI and Digital Policy
description: Annual comparative review of national AI policies, democratic governance, human rights, and rule-of-law alignment.

review_checks: Coverage normalized as country-level comparative data
Useful for evaluation context rather than canonical statements
known_gaps: Model country scores separately from statement records

live.meta_description: The CAIDP AI and Democratic Values Index evaluates national AI policies against democratic governance, human rights, and rule of law principles. AI Index

title: Stanford AI Index Report
description: Annual report and supporting analysis on AI trends, including policy, regulation, public opinion, and technical indicators.

review_checks: Can enrich policy context and trend metadata
Should remain separate from canonical policy sources
known_gaps: Avoid mixing trend indicators with statement provenance

live.meta_description: The mission of the AI Index is to provide unbiased, rigorously vetted, and globally sourced data for policymakers, researchers, journalists, executives, and the general public to develop a deeper understanding of the complex field of AI.
